Internet Service Providers are facing pressure to maintain user anonymity and data security.

New technologies are being developed to give users more control over their personal information.
Third-party data brokers continue to compile detailed profiles of millions of Americans without their knowledge or explicit permission.
Foreign governments have been accused of accessing private communications through backdoors built into popular messaging applications and social platforms.
Negative consequences of data mining include targeted manipulation, identity theft, and unauthorized profiling of individual consumer behaviors.
Legislation, such as the GDPR, imposes strict regulations on companies, significantly affecting how they handle consumer data.
Digital footprints can reveal extensive personal information, highlighting the need for privacy awareness.
